From Figure 2:

    *   mixing pipe
    *   dome

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   High pressure tests can be operated with pressures up to **40 bars.**
*   Maximum air mass flow of **1.3 kg/s**.
*   Air can be electically preheated up to **1000 K**.
*   Optical windows size: **160x140 mm²**
*   Combustion chamber axial length: **170 mm**
*   Combustion chamber radial window distance: **100 mm**
*   Combustion chamber frame distance: **111 mm**
*   Exhaust nozzle outlet diameter: **50 mm**
*   Enhanced FLOX® burner consists of **12** single nozzles
*   Pitch circle diameter of nozzles: **84.5 mm**
*   Aspect ratio height to width = **1/2** for a rectangular inlet channel

title: FLOX_Combustion_at_High_Power_Density_and_High_Flame_Temperatures

Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   λ = 1.6 (equivalence ratio)
*   H+O+M→OH*+M

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Operating condition overview. Range or maximum value for pressures p, nozzle exit velocities Unozzle, air preheat temperature T, air mass flows Qair, natural gas mass flows QNG, H₂ and C3H8 fuel content f, and thermal power (absolute value P, per area pressure PA, and per volume pressure Pv) for enhanced FLOX® burner versions 2A and 2D**

    | Header                       | Units       | 2A      | 2D      |
    | :---------------------------- | :---------- | :------ | :------ |
    | Φnozzle                      | mm        | 12      | 10      |
    | p                           | bar       | 5-7     | 7      |
    | Unozzle                      | m/s       | 70-150  | 90-133  |
    | T                           | K         | 673-773 | 673     |
    | Qair                        | g/s       | <660    | <460    |
    | QNG                         | g/s       | <26     | <19     |
    | f(H₂)                       | vol %     | 0-50    | 0-100   |
    | f(C3H8)                      | vol %     | 0       | 0-20    |
    | P                           | MW        | 0.6-1.3 | <1      |
    | PA                           | MW/m² bar | 10-23   | <16     |
    | Pv                           | MW/m³ bar | 53-117  | <81     |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 5, Combustor**:  Includes the labels: Combustor (with quartz glass walls for optical access)
*   **Fig. 6, Schematic of setup for PIV measurements:**  Includes the following components: Convex lens, beam expansion (cylindrical lenses), Nd:YAG Laser, Filter 532 nm, CCD, Combustor

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Nozzle length: 28 mm
*   FLOX burner 2A nozzle diameter: 12 mm (dump ratio 6.4)
*   FLOX burner 2D nozzle diameter: 10 mm (dump ratio 9.2)
*   Operating pressure: p = 7 bars
*   Nozzle exit velocity: vaozzle = 90 m/s
*   Air preheat temperature: T = 673 K
*   Adiabatic flame temperature: 1723°C (1996 K)
*   Area specific power density: P = 13.3 MW/m² bar
*   UV Interference Filter wavelength: λ = 312 ± 20 nm
*   CCD gate time: 40 µs
*   Laser wavelength (PIV): λ = 532 nm
*   Laser pulse energy (PIV): 120 mJ
*   Laser pulse duration (PIV): 5 ns
*   Cylindrical lenses focal lengths (PIV): f₁=-12.

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 7: Arrangement of burner and measurement plane for configurations of 0 deg and 15 deg**
    *   Diagram shows a circular burner cross-section with fuel injectors arranged around the perimeter.
    *   A plane ("laser sheet") is shown passing through the burner.
    *   Coordinate axes x, y, z are denoted.

*   **Figure 8: Cross sectional view of the combustion chamber with the enhanced FLOX burner.**
    *   Components - Pump laser (ring on the left side), Stokes laser (spot on the left side), CARS signal (narrow outer ring on the right side)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Filter wavelength: 532 nm
*   Particle diameter: 1 µm (titanium dioxide particles)
*   PIV system recording rate: 5 double frames/s
*   PIV laser pulse separation: 4 µs to 8 µs
*   Combustion chamber temperature: >1600 °C
*   Interrogation window size: 64 x 64 pixel (corresponding to a resolution of 7 x 7 mm^2)
*   Vertical direction denoted as height h = z
*   x=y=z=0 mm - intersection point of burner central axis and plane 12mm from burner front plate
*   0 deg configuration:
    *   z range: -25 mm < z < -5 mm
    *    x range: 0 mm < x < 140 mm
*   15 deg configuration:
    *   z range: -5 mm < z < 20 mm
    *   x range: -5 mm < x < 140 mm
*   CARS laser pump wavelength: λ=532 nm.
*  CARS Stokes laser wavelength: λ=607 nm
*   CARS single laser pulse duration: 10 ns
*   CARS laser repetition rate: 10 Hz
*  Spatial resolution of CARS signal: L95% = 4.2 mm
*   CARS probe volume diameter: 300 μm

title: FLOX_Combustion_at_High_Power_Density_and_High_Flame_Temperatures
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Spatial resolution: L95%=4.2 mm

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 9**: Flame position and size for different fuels and air equivalence ratios for enhanced FLOX burner, version 2A.
    *   **(a) CL 15°:** No defined axises here, just labels indicating dimensions in mm. Indicates 12mm thickness above the fuel injectors, and 100mm length overall.
    *   **(b) nozzle positions**: A schematic illustrating the multiple nozzles.
    *   **(c) CL, NG:** x-axis: x [mm], z-axis: z [mm], Curve shows a flame, lambda = 1.6 indicated above the flame.
    *  **(d) CL, NG + H2:** x-axis: x [mm], z-axis: z [mm], Curves show two flame configurations. Lambda = 1.6 and lambda = 2.0 curves. Z axis goes from 0 to 20 (mm), X axis goes from 0 to 120 (mm).

*   **Figure 10**: Frequency spectra of pressure dynamics (information found in text, no image provided)
    *   Nozzle exit velocity of Unozzle = 120 m/s
    *   Nozzle exit velocity of Unozzle = 70 m/s. Periodic peaks at 50 Hz, 150 Hz, etc. Amplitude rises to 20.7 mbars at Unozzle = 70 m/s.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 9 (a):** Configuration of the combustor; 15 deg, indication of the size and the position of the chemiluminescence image plane;
    *   Components: Burner. Labels: Dimensions in mm are indicated (12mm vertical height, 100mm along the chamber)
*   **Figure 9 (b):** Front view of the burner, projection of the positions of the nozzles. Schematics of the burner nozzles.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Optical fiber core diameter: 600 μm
*   Optical fiber length: 20 m
*   Temperature steps for theoretical spectra: 50 K
*   Temperature accuracy (CARS): ±3% (single shot measurements), ± 5% (mean temperatures)
*   Spatial resolution (L95% = 4.2 mm (without flame), 5-10 mm (with flame)
*   Burner pressure (p): 7 bars
*   Nozzle exit velocity (U nozzle): 90 m/s, 120 m/s, 70 m/s
*   Air preheat temperature (T): 673 K
*   Lambda (λ): 1.6, 2.0.
*   Frequency peak: 634 Hz.
*   Amplitude (presssure pulsations): rises to 20.7 mbars at v nozzle = 70 m/s.

title: FLOX_Combustion_at_High_Power_Density_and_High_Flame_Temperatures
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 9]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the image provided:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   p = 7 bars
*   U nozzle = 133 m/s
*   T = 673 K
*   P_A > 10 MW/m^2 bar
*   T_ad = 2000 K

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Title:**  Measured NOX values for enhanced FLOX® burner, version 2D, for different fuel mixtures (natural gas, natural gas + propane, natural gas + hydrogen)
*   **Axes:**
    *   x-axis: Adiabatic flame temperature Tad [K] from 1400 to 2200
    *   y-axis: NOX dry, at 15% O2 [ppm] from 0 to 20
*   **Curves:**
    *   NG (Natural Gas)
    *   89% H2
    *   50% H2
    *   10% Propan (Propane)
    *   20% Propan (Propane)
*   **Key Values:**  Data points for each curve show NOX emissions as a function of adiabatic flame temperature.

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers/Constants/Parameters:**

*   Nozzle exit velocity: up to 150 m/s
*   Preheat Temperature: 673 K.
*   Pressure: up to 7 bars.
*   Adiabatic Flame Temperature: 2000 K.
*   NOx values below 10 ppm

**Nomenclature:**

*   Ønozzle = nozzle diameter (mm)
*   Unozzle = nozzle exit velocity (m/s)
*   p = pressure (bar)
*   λ local = local air equivalence ratio
*   λ global = global air equivalence ratio
*   T = air preheat temperature (K)
*   P = thermal power (MW)
*   PA = thermal power density per area (MW/m² bar)
*   Pv = thermal power density per volume (MW/m³ bar)
*   Tad = adiabatic flame temperature (K)
*   Tmax = maximum local temperature (K)
*   Ω = mass flow (g/s)
*   Qair = air mass flow (g/s)
*   QNG = natural gas mass flow (g/s)
*   f(H₂) = fuel mixture content, hydrogen H₂+NG (vol %)
*   f(C3H8) = fuel mixture content, propane C3H8+NG (vol %)
*   λ = wavelength (nm)
*   f = focal length (mm)
*   x,y,z,h = coordinates (mm)
*   Tmean = mean temperature (K)
*   Tmopro = most probable temperature (K)
*   L95% = spatial resolution (mm)
*   N= number of evaluated spectra
